title:
title
how are people evaluating ai trading systems vs traditional algotrading approaches
author: Disastrous_Way_3396
selftext:
body  
i have been reading more into ai based trading systems lately and how they compare to traditional algorithmic trading setups.  
  
a lot of older algotrading approaches seem heavily rule based like fixed indicators, predefined entry logic, and strict execution conditions, while newer ai driven systems are being described as more adaptive with real time market analysis and dynamic risk adjustments.  
  
what i find interesting is that both approaches still depend heavily on how the underlying strategy is designed and how risk is managed, not just the technology itself.  
  
some systems also seem to combine ai analysis with human oversight for monitoring and adjustments, which feels like a middle ground between full automation and manual control. i discover across a few discussions around managed execution setups like bulkquant while researching this space, and it made me curious how people here actually evaluate these tools in practice.  
  
for those with experience in algotrading or ai assisted investing systems, what do you think matters more in real performance: strategy design, execution infrastructure, or ongoing monitoring and adjustment?  
  
Do you see ai systems actually improving decision quality, or are they mainly just faster execution layers on top of traditional logic?
